content.rendered:
DYI or hire a handyman? The typical Appliance Installation Cost will run between $350-$600. While the initial cost of the appliance is a significant expense, it is essential to remember that installation costs can add up quickly. The price can increase quite a bit if you need to run wiring, update plumbing or add ductwork.

Factors of Appliance Installation Cost

Expect to pay even more if the installer needs to alter the cabinets or countertops. However, as long as the space has all the right hookups, the installation process can be relatively straightforward. In this outline, we will explore the costs associated with installing these home appliances and the factors that can impact the overall cost.

  • Factors of Appliance Installation Cost
    • Average Kitchen Appliance Installation cost
      • Electric Cooktop
      • Gas Cooktop
      • Ranges & Ovens
      • Dishwasher
      • Refrigerator
      • Range Hood
      • Microwave
      • Kitchen Exhaust Fan
    • Kitchen Appliance Packages
    • Washer & Dryer Installation
    • Cost of Installing Stainless Steel Appliances
    • Smart Appliance Installation Pricing
    • Labor Cost to Install Appliances

Average Kitchen Appliance Installation cost

On average, you can anticipate paying $250 for the professional installation of each appliance you require. If you make a substantial purchase from a home improvement store, the installation cost may be included in the appliance’s price. However, if you purchase custom appliances or from a retailer that doesn’t offer delivery, you may need to enlist a professional to install them. The difficulty of installation varies depending on the type of appliance, and every kitchen or laundry room appliance requires its own unique considerations.

Electric Cooktop

To replace an existing electric cooktop, the cost can range from $450 to $4,000. This includes the appliance and expert installation, which typically costs $250 to $600. If alterations need to be made to the countertop or new wiring and outlets are required, the total cost may increase.

Gas Cooktop

A gas cooktop install costs $430 to $4,225 if the space does not need any extra work. The costs include a $320 to $4,030 cooktop and $60 connection kit plus $250 to $600 for the install. The price goes up if the installer has to run new gas lines and a shut off valve or needs to cut out the countertop.

Ranges & Ovens

Installing a freestanding or drop-in range typically costs between $515 and $7,625. This includes the cost of the range itself, which can range from $410 to $7,500, and installation services, which typically cost $200 to $600. If you prefer a double wall oven to accompany your cooktop, you can expect to pay between $1,905 and $6,685.

This price includes the cost of the oven, which ranges from $1,800 to $6,560, as well as installation services, which cost between $200 and $600. If you are using gas, you will need to add an additional $60 for a connection kit. Any modifications to the kitchen cabinets and counters will increase the overall cost.

Dishwasher

The cost of installing a dishwasher typically ranges from $700 to $2,400. This includes the cost of the appliance as well as installation services, which cost between $250 to $600. If the installer needs to remove cabinets to fit the dishwasher under the counter, the total cost may increase.

Refrigerator

The cost of installing a refrigerator can range from $615 to $11,100. This includes the cost of the refrigerator itself and installation services, which typically cost between $150 and $600. Higher costs are typically associated with smart refrigerators or those with water dispensers and icemakers.

Range Hood

The cost of installing a range hood ranges from $350 to $2,665, depending on the style and location. This includes the cost of the range hood itself and installation services, which typically cost between $150 to $500. Wall-mount models are typically more expensive and difficult to install compared to under-cabinet ones. If cabinet removal is required, the total cost will increase.

Microwave

The cost of installing a microwave ranges from $550 to $2,500. This includes the cost of the microwave itself, which can range from $50 to $2,300, and installation services, which typically cost between $150 to $500. Under-cabinet microwaves are typically more expensive to purchase and install compared to built-in models.

Kitchen Exhaust Fan

The cost of installing a basic kitchen exhaust fan ranges from $450 to $2,000. This includes the cost of the fan itself and installation services, which typically cost between $150 to $500. If the system requires new ductwork, wiring, or a switch, the price may increase.

Kitchen Appliance Packages

Kitchen appliance packages cost $1,360 to $19,050, depending on the models you select and where you have them installed. Expect to pay $1,100 to $18,500 for a fridge, range and dishwasher package along with $400 to $1000 to install. Retailers often reduce the price by up to $1,800 when you buy three or more appliances.

Washer & Dryer Installation

If all hookups and vents are already in place, the cost of installing a washer and dryer is between $150 to $350. Installing a washing machine, which typically costs between $500 to $2,500, can range from $495 to $2,475 for installation. Similarly, installing a clothes dryer, which costs between $445 to $2,100, can range from $495 to $2,275 for installation. The total cost to buy and install both machines typically ranges from $990 to $4,400.

If new hookups are needed, expect to add $350 to $2,000 to the total cost. Installing a new 120V circuit typically costs between $75 to $250, while a 240V circuit can cost between $300 to $800. Plumbing typically costs between $100 to $200, and dryer duct installation costs $150 or more depending on complexity. Additional dryer duct cleaning costs an average of $130.

If you choose to place your washers and dryers in the kitchen, the plumbing and electrical work may be simpler. However, if you want to hide the appliances when they’re not in use with new cabinets or closet space, this will add to the job. A complete kitchen remodel costs an average of $22,500.

Cost of Installing Stainless Steel Appliances

Installing smart appliances can cost anywhere from $850 to $8,500, depending on the appliance and installation needs. This includes $150 to $600 for installation services. Smart appliances typically cost 20% to 50% more than their basic counterparts. Smart technology can be used in refrigerators, microwaves, washers, dryers, ovens, and dishwashers.

When selecting a professional for installation, it is essential to choose someone who understands how to set up smart appliances and can assist you in utilizing their apps and other functions. Most smart home innovations have an app that allows remote control of the appliances. Check the compatibility of your appliances with apps from companies like Amazon, Google, Apple, Nest, and Pebble.

Smart Appliance Installation Pricing

Installing smart appliances can cost anywhere from $850 to $8,500, depending on the appliance and installation needs. This includes $150 to $600 for installation services. Smart appliances typically cost 20% to 50% more than their basic counterparts. Smart technology can be used in refrigerators, microwaves, washers, dryers, ovens, and dishwashers.

When selecting a professional for installation, it is essential to choose someone who understands how to set up smart appliances and can assist you in utilizing their apps and other functions. Most smart home innovations have an app that allows remote control of the appliances. Check the compatibility of your appliances with apps from companies like Amazon, Google, Apple, Nest, and Pebble.

Labor Cost to Install Appliances

If you’re hiring a local appliance installer, like tOdd-Jobs, the typical cost for their services ranges from $70 to $500. The installation fee may vary depending on whether your home requires structural modifications or additional hookups. In most cases, appliance installers charge a flat rate depending on the type of appliance that needs to be installed.
